issues.title: PermissionGate JSX truncated, RequestAccessPage missing closing tags and ARIA
issues.description: The RequestAccessPage component's JSX is truncated mid-render (ends at a div opening). The component cannot compile. It also lacks aria-live region for the denied message and the PermissionDenied inline component lacks role='alert'.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx, in the RequestAccessPage component: BEFORE: The JSX is truncated after the opening div — the component body is incomplete, causing a build failure. The PermissionDenied component lacks role='alert'. AFTER: Complete the RequestAccessPage component with a full render: ShieldAlert icon (size 48, color textTertiary), heading 'Access Restricted' (h2, fontSize 2xl, fontWeight semibold, color textPrimary), paragraph showing the moduleName or 'this section' and the user's current role, and a 'Go to Dashboard' button using navigate('/'). Add role='alert' and aria-live='polite' to the PermissionDenied wrapper div. Ensure all JSX tags are properly closed. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: ProtectedRoute SkeletonLoader and component body truncated
issues.description: The ProtectedRoute component's SkeletonLoader return JSX and the main ProtectedRoute function body are truncated. The file ends mid-component, meaning protected routes cannot render, breaking the entire app routing.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx, in the ProtectedRoute component: BEFORE: The SkeletonLoader's return JSX and the main ProtectedRoute default export are truncated — the file ends before the component logic that checks auth state, shows loading skeleton, redirects to /login if unauthenticated, checks permissions, and renders children. AFTER: Complete the SkeletonLoader by closing its return JSX properly. Then complete the ProtectedRoute component: (1) call useAuth() to get user, loading, initialized; (2) call usePermissions(); (3) if not initialized or loading, return SkeletonLoader with ariaLabel='Loading application'; (4) if no user, return Navigate to='/login' with state={{ returnTo: location.pathname }}; (5) determine requiredPerm from requiredPermission prop or MODULE_PERMISSIONS[moduleId]; (6) if requiredPerm and not hasPermission(requiredPerm), return RequestAccessPage with moduleName prop; (7) otherwise render DevBanner (if isDevBypassActive()) plus children. Export ProtectedRoute as default. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Login page missing loading state, error display, and form validation on blur
issues.description: The Login page (per PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA AUTH-001 through AUTH-007) must show a loading spinner on the submit button during auth, display mapped error messages (not raw Supabase errors), validate email format on blur, and disable the submit button while loading. Current implementation likely lacks these states based on the auth store pattern.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, in the Login page component: BEFORE: The login form submit button does not show a loading/disabled state during authentication, error messages may display raw Supabase error strings, and there is no onBlur email validation. AFTER: (1) Add a local isSubmitting state that is set true before calling signIn and false in finally block. (2) Disable the submit button and show a spinner icon (Loader2 from lucide-react with spin animation) when isSubmitting is true — button width must not change (use min-width). (3) Add an emailError state; on email input blur, validate with a regex (/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/) and set emailError to 'Please enter a valid email address' if invalid; show red border and error text below input. (4) Map auth error messages: if error includes 'invalid' or 'credentials' show 'Email or password is incorrect'; if 'rate limit' show 'Too many attempts, try again shortly'; if 'fetch' or 'network' show 'Unable to connect, check your internet'; otherwise show the error as is. Never display 'AuthApiError' or 'invalid_credentials' raw strings. (5) Add aria-invalid and aria-describedby to inputs when errors are present. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Login page not responsive below 480px, touch targets undersized
issues.description: Per Global Acceptance Standards, every page must be usable at 375px. The login form likely uses fixed widths or padding that causes horizontal overflow on small screens. Input heights and button heights must be at least 44px for touch targets per Apple HIG.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Login.tsx, in the Login page component: BEFORE: The login form container and inputs may use fixed pixel widths that overflow on 375px screens. Input and button heights may be below the 44px touch target minimum. AFTER: (1) Set the form container to maxWidth 400px, width '100%', padding '16px' on mobile (use a media query check via window.innerWidth or a responsive padding: clamp(16px, 4vw, 48px)). (2) Set all input elements to height 48px (min 44px), width '100%', fontSize 16px (prevents iOS zoom on focus). (3) Set the submit button to height 48px, width '100%', fontSize 16px, fontWeight 600. (4) Add margin bottom 16px between form fields. (5) Ensure the logo/header area does not push the form below the fold on a 667px height screen — use a max logo height of 40px on mobile. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: queryKeys.ts file truncated, missing closing brace breaks all TanStack Query caching
issues.description: The queryKeys factory object is truncated after schedulePhases — the file ends without closing the object and the export. This causes a TypeScript parse error that would break any file importing queryKeys, cascading across the app.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/queryKeys.ts, in the queryKeys object: BEFORE: The object literal ends after the schedulePhases entry without a closing brace or semicolon — the file is truncated. AFTER: Add the following missing entries and close the object properly: (1) Add safetyInspections with all: (projectId: string) => ['safety_inspections', projectId] as const. (2) Add notifications with all: ['notifications'] as const and unread: ['notifications', 'unread'] as const. (3) Add weather with byProject: (projectId: string) => ['weather', projectId] as const. (4) Add paymentApplications with all: (projectId: string) => ['payment_applications', projectId] as const. (5) Close the queryKeys object with '} as const;'. Ensure the file ends with a valid export or that the const is already exported. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore's auth is enterprise-grade with SSO (SAML/SCIM), org-level role management, and granular per-tool permissions enforced server-side. Fieldwire uses simple per-project roles with magic link login optimized for field crews. SiteSync's auth module has the right architectural concepts (PermissionGate, ProtectedRoute, role hierarchy, RLS) but critical files are truncated and non-functional, meaning the entire permission system is effectively bypassed in production. No competitor would ship with broken route protection or incomplete permission gate components.
summary: The Authentication & Authorization module has sound architectural decisions — a PermissionGate component, ProtectedRoute wrapper, role-based permission hooks, tenant guard middleware, and query key factory — but two of the three core files (PermissionGate.tsx and ProtectedRoute.tsx) are truncated mid-JSX, making them unable to compile. This means protected routes do not actually protect anything, and permission gates cannot render. The queryKeys.ts file is also truncated, breaking TanStack Query caching app-wide. The Login page lacks loading states, mapped error messages, blur validation, and mobile responsiveness — all explicit requirements from PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA (AUTH-001 through AUTH-007). The five surgical fixes target: completing the PermissionGate with ARIA attributes, completing the ProtectedRoute with full auth flow logic, adding login form states and error mapping, making the login responsive for field use at 375px, and closing the queryKeys factory to restore query caching.
